content_of_post_to_answer: Why do successful people see failure as an opportunity instead of a defeat?
answer: Successful people often don't see failure as a verdict because they have learned to separate the result from the identity of the person creating it. A failed attempt becomes information about a strategy, timing, skill gap, or assumption. The overlooked shift is that failure only feels like defeat when we treat it as proof that we are incapable. When treated as data, it becomes part of the process of making better decisions.
